tickets.title: Create hooks.d directory structure
tickets.description: Create the hooks.d directory structure for ralph. On ralph-init, create a hooks.d/ directory in the project with subdirectories for each hook event: hooks.d/on-start/, hooks.d/on-complete/, hooks.d/on-error/. Each directory should contain a .gitkeep file to ensure it's tracked. Update ralph-init to create this structure.

tickets.title: Create ralph-hooks executor script
tickets.description: Create bin/ralph-hooks script that executes all executable scripts in a given hooks.d/<event>/ directory. Usage: ralph-hooks <event> [json-payload]. The script should: 1) Find all executable files in hooks.d/<event>/, 2) Execute each in lexicographic order, 3) Pass context via stdin (JSON) and environment variables (RALPH_HOOK_EVENT=event name, RALPH_HOOK_CONTEXT=full JSON), 4) Continue on failure (log errors but don't abort), 5) Report collected errors at end. Follows hooks.d pattern from ../specs/hooks.d.md.

tickets.title: Implement on-start hook in ralph/ralph-once
tickets.description: Modify bin/ralph and bin/ralph-once to call 'ralph-hooks on-start' with ticket context before starting the docker container. The JSON payload should include: {"event": "start", "ticket": <current-ticket-json>, "timestamp": <iso8601>}. Run on host (not in container) so hooks can access host tools like todo CLI. Depends on ticket #2.

tickets.title: Implement on-complete hook
tickets.description: Modify bin/ralph and bin/ralph-once to call 'ralph-hooks on-complete' on host after docker exits successfully. Payload: {"event": "complete", "ticket": <completed-ticket-json>, "summary": <last-progress.txt-entry>, "timestamp": <iso8601>}. Must detect successful completion (check exit code and/or ticket status change) and extract the last progress.txt entry. Runs on host so hooks can access host tools. Depends on ticket #2.

tickets.title: Implement on-error hook
tickets.description: Modify bin/ralph and bin/ralph-once to call 'ralph-hooks on-error' on host when docker exits non-zero or ticket completion cannot be detected. Payload: {"event": "error", "ticket": <ticket-json>, "exit_code": <code>, "timestamp": <iso8601>}. Runs on host so hooks can access host tools. Depends on ticket #2.

tickets.title: Add example hook scripts
tickets.description: Create example hooks in hooks.d/ (not executable by default, use .example extension). Include: 1) on-complete/log-completion.sh.example - appends ticket summary to a log file, demonstrates parsing JSON payload. 2) on-error/notify.sh.example - sends desktop notification on failure (notify-send or similar). Comments in each should explain how to enable (chmod +x, rename) and how to adapt for other tools. Depends on ticket #1.

tickets.title: Document hooks.d in ralph-help
tickets.description: Update bin/ralph-help to document the hooks.d system. Include: available events (on-start, on-complete, on-error), hook directory structure, payload format for each event, how to enable a hook (make executable), example use cases. Depends on tickets #1-5.

tickets.title: Add ralph hooks CLI management
tickets.description: Add hooks management subcommands to ralph. Commands: 'ralph hooks list [event]' - list all hooks or hooks for specific event, 'ralph hooks add <event> <script>' - create symlink to script in hooks.d/<event>/, 'ralph hooks rm <event> <name>' - remove hook. Follows CLI management pattern from ../specs/hooks.d.md. Depends on ticket #1.

tickets.title: Move iteration loop from container to host
tickets.description: Refactor bin/ralph to handle the iteration loop on host instead of inside docker. Currently ralph-loop.sh runs multiple tickets inside one docker session, so hooks only fire once when docker exits. Change: 1) bin/ralph loops on host, calling docker once per ticket (like ralph-once does), 2) ralph-loop.sh becomes single-ticket (or removed, use ralph-once.sh), 3) Hooks fire after each ticket since docker exits each time. This ensures on-start/on-complete/on-error fire per-ticket. Preserve max_iter limit.

tickets.title: Move ralph state to .ralph/ directory
tickets.description: Refactor to consolidate ralph state files into .ralph/ subdirectory. Move: tickets.json -> .ralph/tickets.json, progress.txt -> .ralph/progress.txt, hooks.d/ -> .ralph/hooks.d/. Keep RALPH.md and CLAUDE.md at project root. Update all bin/ scripts (ralph, ralph-once, ralph-init, ralph-hooks, ralph-add, ralph-tickets) to use .ralph/ paths. ralph-init should create .ralph/ structure instead of root-level files. Add fallback logic: check .ralph/ first, fall back to root for backwards compat. Add 'ralph migrate' subcommand that moves existing root-level files into .ralph/ for a project.

tickets.title: Smoke test: create test.md
tickets.description:
Create a file called test.md in the project root with the content '# Smoke Test
This file was created by ralph as a smoke test.' Then commit it. This is a throwaway ticket to verify the pipeline works.

tickets.title: Use sandcastle RunResult for structured commit handling
tickets.description: Currently src/sandcastle.ts ignores RunResult from sandcastle.run(). Sandcastle exposes: RunResult.commits (SHAs the agent made), RunResult.preservedWorktreePath (set when agent finished but left uncommitted changes), RunResult.completionSignal, branchStrategy: 'merge-to-head' (squashes iteration to single commit). Idea: prompt agent to emit '<commit>feat: msg\n\nbody</commit>' as completion signal; ralph parses it and either uses sandcastle's squash-merge or reshapes commits into one well-formed conventional commit per ticket. Also detect preservedWorktreePath case (agent forgot to commit changes) and either auto-commit or surface to user. Goal: clean git history per ticket instead of N feat+chore commits.

tickets.title: Add --log flag to write iteration logs to .ralph/logs/
tickets.description:
Sandcastle supports two logging modes via 'logging' option (see node_modules/@ai-hero/sandcastle/dist/run.d.ts: LoggingOption): { type: 'stdout' } (interactive TUI, current default in src/sandcastle.ts:43) or { type: 'file', path: string } (writes agent + iteration progress to a log file). Today ralph hardcodes stdout, so headless/background runs lose detailed agent output once the TUI scrolls or terminal closes.

GOAL: opt-in --log flag on 'ralph loop' and 'ralph once' that drains sandcastle output to per-iteration log files under .ralph/logs/.

SCOPE:
1. cli.ts: add boolean option 'log' to both 'loop' and 'once' command definitions. Thread the value through the command handlers.
2. state.ts: add 'logsDir: join(root, "logs")' to StatePaths and resolveState() (keeps log path derivation alongside other state paths).
3. sandcastle.ts: change runRalphContainer signature to accept { logFile?: string }. When logFile set, use logging: { type: 'file', path: logFile }; otherwise keep current stdout mode.
4. loop.ts / once.ts: when --log is set, mkdir -p state.logsDir, build a per-iteration filename, pass to runRalphContainer. Print 'Logging iteration N to: <path>' to stdout before invoking so user can 'tail -f'.
5. Filename pattern: '<ISO-timestamp>-iter<N>-ticket<ID>.log' for loop; '<ISO-timestamp>-ticket<ID>.log' for once. Keeps logs sortable + identifiable.
6. ralph init: add '.ralph/logs/' to the generated .gitignore (or whatever it currently writes for .ralph/) so logs don't get committed by accident. Verify init.ts doesn't already exclude them.
7. Tests:
   - state.test.ts: assert resolveState includes logsDir
   - loop / once: small unit covering log-path construction; full integration not needed
   - hooks-payloads / dispatch unaffected

NON-GOALS:
- Don't change the default; --log stays opt-in. Interactive devs keep the TUI.
- Don't expose the rest of LoggingOption (sandcastle's default 'file' would auto-place under .sandcastle/logs/ — we want .ralph/logs/ so it's colocated with tickets/progress).

VERIFY:
- 'ralph once --log' creates .ralph/logs/<ts>-ticket<ID>.log with the agent's full output
- 'ralph loop 3 --log' creates 3 separate files
- Without --log: behavior unchanged, TUI renders as today
- bun run typecheck && bun vitest run pass

REFS:
- node_modules/@ai-hero/sandcastle/dist/run.d.ts — LoggingOption + RunResult.logFilePath
- src/sandcastle.ts:43 — current hardcoded { type: 'stdout' }
- src/loop.ts:35, src/once.ts:21 — runRalphContainer call sites
